location.name: Maimunarnika
location.description: The legendary open-air bar in Borisova Gradina — beer under the trees, a stage in the middle of the park, and the whole summer evening around it.
location.directions: Inside Borisova Gradina park. Easiest on foot from the Eagles' Bridge entrance.

about.intro: A fun evening of beer and informal Java talks at Maimunarnika, Sofia — organized by the Bulgarian Java User Group, Codexio and random beer fans from Bulgaria. There was an agenda published before the event, but everyone was welcome to take the stage and talk. Entrance was free and registration optional — last-minute friends explicitly included.

highlights.title: Beer (and wine!)
highlights.text: Don't drink beer? No problem — there was wine as well, and even french fries… or were they belgian? 🤔

highlights.title: Stand-up over slides
highlights.text: Stand-up-like talks instead of deep technical ones — to keep the attendees' attention where it belongs: on the fun.

highlights.title: Free for everyone
highlights.text: Free entrance, optional registration. Bring your last-minute friends and anyone up for beer and Java talks.

highlights.title: Raffle finale
highlights.text: The evening wrapped up with a raffle by the event organizers.

schedule.title: Opening
schedule.abstract: Welcome talk by Bulgarian Java User Group, Codexio and friends*.

schedule.title: Can Java Ever Replace Python in AI
schedule.speaker: Stefan Angelov

schedule.title: Rebuilding in Motion: Unpacking the Monolith into Microservices Without Hitting Pause on Product Delivery
schedule.speaker: Aleksander Aleksandrov

schedule.title: You were obsolete long before AI
schedule.speaker: Ivan Yonkov

schedule.title: I hope AI is going to replace me
schedule.speaker: Ivaylo Zhelev

schedule.title: Bad developers write awful prompts, so you still have a chance
schedule.speaker: Yoana Borissova
